title: General Program Manager
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:





We are seeking a dynamic and talented General Program Manager to join our team. This role is perfect for a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare.



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We're seeking a super smart, hungry, and resilient individual who is passionate about technology and healthcare to join us as a Program Manager. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and brings a strong organizational mindset to complex projects. You will be responsible for overseeing multiple complex programs across various departments to ensure successful delivery within scope, timeline, and budget. This role requires a self-motivated individual who can work independently and adapt to the dynamic nature of a growing startup in the healthcare technology space.



Responsibilities

- Impact the strategy and execution for AnsibleHealth's general operations, clinical operations, and technology initiatives, working closely with cross-functional teams.

- Work independently on projects through the full life cycle, overseeing clinical, operational, and technology aspects.

- Develop and manage program plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocation to ensure successful delivery.

- Define program scope, objectives, and success criteria in collaboration with stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

- Establish program governance structures and processes, including risk management, issue management, and change management.

- Monitor and report on program progress, status, and performance to stakeholders and executive leadership.

- Ensure effective communication and alignment with stakeholders at all levels.

- Continuously identify and mitigate program risks and issues.

- Facilitate program-related meetings and workshops.

- Develop and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders to drive program success and identify growth opportunities.

- Bring a continuous improvement mindset to our operations to ensure we're ready to scale.

- Anticipate issues, propose solutions, manage dependencies, and identify areas for tool/process improvement.

- Collaborate with and steer internal teams, including Marketing, Engineering, Program Management, and Clinical Operations.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable to be in a constantly changing role with a high level of ambiguity - as we are a fast-moving startup operating in an ever-evolving environment. He or she should have excellent analytical, communication and management skills to succeed in this role.



Education

Bachelor's degree from one of the top five universities in the Philippines.



Required Qualifications and competencies

- Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; 0-5 years of relevant work experience is an advantage but not required.

- Demonstrated passion for technology and ability to quickly adapt to new digital tools and platforms.

- Exceptional organizational skills.

- Resilient mindset with the ability to bounce back from challenges and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

- Keen interest in healthcare and improving patient outcomes; prior healthcare experience is a plus but not mandatory.

- High intellectual curiosity and a hunger for personal and professional development.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and creatively.

- Proficiency in Google Workspace and willingness to learn new software tools as needed.

- Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

- Adaptable and comfortable with ambiguity in a startup environment.

- Eagerness to take on leadership roles and initiative to drive projects forward.

- Ability to influence and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.

- Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.

- Enthusiasm for continuous learning and process improvement.



The ideal candidate is a highly intelligent, motivated individual who is passionate about leveraging technology to make a difference in healthcare. We're looking for someone who can bring fresh perspectives, adapt quickly to new challenges, and contribute to our innovative and fast-paced work environment.



Job Details

Full-time: 40 hours a week, with flexible PTO and healthcare benefits.

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work Eastern hours)

title: Marketing Coordinator
description:
# Marketing Coordinator — GHL Operations & Campaign Execution | Fully Remote | US-Based SaaS Company



We're 
 — a fast-growing US-based software company that builds growth management tools for vacation rental operators. Our clients are property managers across the United States who rely on us to help them grow through data, automation, and smart marketing.



We're hiring **2 Marketing Coordinators** to own the technical execution layer of our client operations. This is a behind-the-scenes role — you won't be on Zoom calls with clients, but every campaign our clients launch, every automation that fires, and every system that runs smoothly will be because of you.



**If you know GoHighLevel inside and out and you love building systems that work, this role was made for you.**



---



### What You'll Do



- **Own GoHighLevel.** You are the GHL expert on the team. Sub-account setup, domain configuration, workflow automations, pipelines,
 sequences, SMS campaigns, calendar configuration, landing pages — all of it runs through you.

- **Build and launch marketing campaigns.** Account Coordinators will hand you client specs and meeting notes. You take those and build the campaigns in GHL —
 drip sequences, direct mail triggers, contact segmentation, pipeline automations. Then you test everything before it goes live.

- **Set up and maintain client accounts.** Domain purchases,
 domain authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), warm-up protocols, A2P registration, rebilling configuration, user access — you make sure every sub-account is built right from day one.

- **Import and manage contact lists.** Clients build targeted prospect lists in our data platform (OwnerPoint). You import those lists into ContactPoint, apply the right tags, exclude suppression lists, and make sure segmentation is clean.

- **QA everything.** Before any campaign goes live, you review it. Workflows trigger correctly?
 timing right? Merge fields populate? Exclusion logic working? You're the last checkpoint before the client sees it.

- **Build templates and systems.** We're scaling fast. You'll create standardized workflow templates, pipeline templates,
 templates, and campaign blueprints that make every future setup faster and more consistent.

- **Audit and troubleshoot.** Diagnose issues in existing accounts — broken automations, deliverability problems, misconfigured triggers. Fix what you can, escalate to GHL support or Extendly when needed, and follow through until it's resolved.

- **Create Loom walkthroughs.** Record short video explanations of setups, features, or processes that Account Coordinators can share with clients. You won't be on client calls, but your Loom videos will be.

- **Receive and execute task handoffs.** Account Coordinators will assign work through HubSpot tickets after client meetings. You pick up the technical tasks, execute them accurately, and close the ticket. Fast turnaround matters.

- **Communicate with the team.** Daily coordination with Account Coordinators, participation in team syncs, proactive updates on task progress and blockers. Clear internal communication is essential.



---



### Must-Haves — Non-Negotiable



- **Strong GoHighLevel experience.** This is the #1 requirement. You should be able to build workflows, configure automations, set up pipelines, create
 campaigns, manage calendars, and troubleshoot issues without hand-holding. If you've managed multiple GHL sub-accounts, even better.

- *
 deliverability knowledge.** You understand dedicated sending domains, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, warm-up protocols, list segmentation, and sender reputation management. You've dealt with deliverability issues before and know how to prevent them.

- **Strong English — written and spoken.** This is not a client-facing role, but you'll communicate daily with our US-based team, write internal documentation, and record Loom videos. Your English needs to be clear and professional.

- **Organized and detail-oriented.** You'll manage technical work across many client accounts simultaneously. Naming conventions, file organization, and systematic approaches matter. Sloppy work at scale creates chaos.

- **Self-directed problem solver.** You can take a set of meeting notes and execute without needing every step spelled out. When you hit a wall, you troubleshoot — GHL support, documentation, AI tools, whatever it takes — before asking for help.

- **Testing discipline.** You don't ship work you haven't tested. Every workflow gets a test contact run through it. Every automation gets verified. This is non-negotiable.

- **Available during US business hours.** Full-time position.



### Strong Advantages



- Experience managing multiple GHL sub-accounts for an agency or SaaS company

- CRM experience beyond GHL (HubSpot, Salesforce, Klaviyo)

- Marketing automation (Zapier, n8n, Make, or similar)

- AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) — we use these extensively in our workflows

- HTML/CSS basics for
 template and landing page customization

- SaaS, property management, vacation rental, or real estate background

- Experience creating SOPs and process documentation



### What We'll Teach You



- Our proprietary platforms (OwnerPoint, ContactPoint, TravelPoint)

- The vacation rental / property management industry

- Our internal workflows, n8n automations, and campaign generation systems

- Our specific GHL snapshot, templates, and account architecture

- HubSpot for internal task management and ticketing



---



### About the Role



This is a **support role**, not a client-facing role. You will not be on Zoom calls with clients or managing client relationships directly. You report to the Account Coordination Team Lead and work alongside Account Coordinators who own the client relationships. They handle the conversations; you handle the buildout.



That said — this is not a button-clicking job. We need people who think, who notice when something could be better, who proactively improve systems, and who take ownership of the quality of their work. The best person in this role will eventually know our GHL environment better than anyone on the team.



We're hiring 2 people for this role because we're in a growth phase with a major enterprise partnership launching that will bring dozens of new clients onboard simultaneously. Speed, accuracy, and reliability are critical.



---



### About ZPoint



Built by The Rentalz Company (Denver, CO). We make software for vacation rental managers — tools to find new property owners, automate marketing, and grow portfolios. Small team, growing fast, remote-first. You'll work directly with leadership.



---



### Compensation



Competitive salary based on location, experience, and skill level. Fully remote, full-time.



---



### How to Apply



1. **Brief cover letter** (2–3 paragraphs) — tell us about your GoHighLevel experience and what kind of work you enjoy most.

2. **2-minute Loom video** — walk us through something you've built in GoHighLevel. Show us a workflow, a campaign, an automation — anything that demonstrates your hands-on skill. Screen share preferred.

3. **Resume or CV.**



Applications without the cover letter and Loom video will not be reviewed.

title: Account Manager
description:
Benefits:



Work From Home

Guaranteed $500 USD per week

Additional available if you perform well

Unlimited potential through residual because you earn as long as your customers say with Fleetsmarts



What we do as a company:



We give a fuel card away for free to truckers in the US that have discounts on diesel fuel. We have add-on products that make the sale more attractive to the customer. Customers can get weekly credit lines for their fuel card if they use our factoring service, which is akin to a pay-day loan service for businesses, but we provide this service for free. We also have a service to help reduce the down payment on their insurance. We market to new trucking companies that are just starting out and all our products are designed to help them get going faster than they would be able to on their own.



What you do:



You call warm appointments for truckers who have already expressed interest

You explain how we can help them

95% of people say yes

The key is following up until they actually start using our services







We set up 4 interviews per day in between the hours of 4:00 and 6:00pm

We will choose one new Cat 3 Re-Engager per week.

After one week, if they don’t hit 60% patch rate, they are out.

For 5 weeks, if they hit high patch rate, we rotate them between Hannah and Cat 1s

We promote one each week to Cat 3 leads and replace our worst AM.











Key Responsibilities:

Proactively follow up on abandoned or inactive leads assigned to account managers.

Present and promote fuel card programs, factoring services, and insurance solutions to potential clients.

Assist customers with the activation of fuel cards and ensure a preferred payment method is added.

Offer factoring services and guide customers through the contract signing process to ensure completion and compliance

Qualifications:

Proven experience in sales, customer retention, or outbound calling (required)

Strong communication and negotiation skills

Confident handling high-volume outbound calls

Goal-oriented, persistent, and able to work independently

Organized and comfortable using CRM tools and office software

Positive attitude and ability to build rapport quickly.

Unlimited potential through residual because you earn as long as your customers stay with Fleetsmarts



What we do as a company:



We give a fuel card away for free to truckers in the US that have discounts on diesel fuel. We have add-on products that make the sale more attractive to the customer. Customers can get weekly credit lines for their fuel card if they use our factoring service, which is asking to a pay-day loan service for businesses, but we provide this service for free. We also have a service to help reduce the down payment on their insurance. We market to new trucking companies that are just starting out and all our products are designed to help them get going faster than they would be able to on their own.



What you do:

You call warm appointments for truckers who have already expressed interest

You explain how we can help them

95% of people say yes

The key is following up until they actually start using our services





Key Responsibilities:

Proactively follow up on abandoned or inactive leads assigned to account managers.

Present and promote fuel card programs, factoring services, and insurance solutions to potential clients.

Assist customers with the activation of fuel cards and ensure a preferred payment method is added.

Offer factoring services and guide customers through the contract signing process to ensure completion and compliance



Qualifications:

Proven experience in sales, customer retention, or outbound calling (required)

Strong communication and negotiation skills

Confident handling high-volume outbound calls

Goal-oriented, persistent, and able to work independently

Organized and comfortable using CRM tools and office software

Positive attitude and ability to build rapport quickly.

title: Social Media Manager (Full-Time VA)
description:
We’re hiring a full-time Social Media Manager to take ownership of our social channels across YouTube,
, Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn.



This role is critical to our growth.



Beyond publishing content, this person will drive consistent channel growth and engagement, strengthen brand credibility, and support high-quality B2B inbound lead generation over time.



We’re looking for someone who is highly knowledgeable in social media, stays on top of platform trends, and is proactive in observing, testing, and recommending what’s working — from formats and hooks to posting styles and platform shifts.



Just as important, this role supports a mission-driven brand. We want someone who genuinely cares about the planet, especially the ocean, and is excited to help amplify that mission through content.



This is a VA role, but we’re looking for someone who thinks strategically about growth, trends, and impact, not just posting.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own day-to-day management of YouTube,
, Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn

Publish and optimize content to support growth and inbound lead generation

Repurpose long-form videos into high-performing short-form content (experience with CapCut is a plus)

Write platform-native captions aligned with brand voice and goals

Maintain a consistent posting cadence across all platforms

Stay up to date on platform trends, algorithm changes, formats, and best practices

Proactively recommend content ideas, formats, hooks, and posting strategies based on trends

Test and iterate on content approaches to improve reach and engagement

Schedule and publish content using tools like Sprout Social and Airtable

Monitor comments, DMs, and engagement; flag potential B2B leads or partnership interest

Maintain a content calendar and weekly posting plan

Track performance metrics tied to growth and inbound signals



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Consistent, on-time posting across all platforms

Measurable growth in reach, engagement, and followers

Regular trend-based recommendations that improve performance

Increased inbound messages from relevant brands or decision-makers

Content that builds trust, authority, and long-term inbound interest



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Proven experience managing multiple social media platforms

Strong understanding of short-form video and content trends

Comfortable working with YouTube Shorts, Reels, TikTok, and LinkedIn

Actively follows and analyzes social media trends and algorithm changes

Proactive communicator who brings ideas, not just executes tasks

Clear, professional English writing skills

Organized, reliable, and self-directed

Experience working as a Virtual Assistant or remote tea
mber

Genuine care for the planet, especially ocean health and conservation



NICE TO HAVE (NOT REQUIRED)

Experience supporting founders, CEOs, or B2B brands

Familiarity with LinkedIn thought leadership and B2B content

Basic video trimming or captioning skills

Canva and/or CapCut experience

Background in sustainability, environmental, or mission-driven work



NOTE: Please send your CV and Portfolio Link.

title: Medical Virtual Assistant
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/2475d4d7-7ed7-449e-a350-75eb5484b5db





What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We are looking for a top-tier Patient Care Coordinator to become an integral part of our expanding team. This role is vital in delivering the best care possible to our patients, handling patient communications, and providing essential support to our clinical staff in their mission to offer exceptional care. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exemplary professionalism, excellent English language communication skills, and outstanding organizational abilities with meticulous attention to detail. This position offers an exciting opportunity to contribute significantly to our patient-centric approach in healthcare delivery.



Responsibilities

• Customize initial discussions based on patient needs, ensuring they understand how the program can address their specific health conditions, providing personalized benefits rather than general services.

• Manage inquiries through multiple communication channels (phone,
, etc.), including voi
 inbox management and message routing, providing prompt and accurate responses.

• Process and manage referrals, working towards efficient conversion within a 7-10 day timeframe while managing multiple referral loop processes for a large patient panel.

• Perform comprehensive data entry including clinical survey scores/results, diagnostic tests, remote physiologic monitoring (RPM), and remote therapeutic monitoring (RTM) data streams.

• Schedule and coordinate patient appointments while maintaining communication with patients regarding their care schedule.

• Manage and process inbound and outbound medical records, ensuring proper documentation and organization.

• Maintain accurate EMR records by updating care notes, face sheets, and ensuring the integrity of patient information.

• Attend clinical meetings, take detailed notes, and follow up on action items and project timelines.

• Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of physicians, engineers, designers, and care tea
mbers to continuously improve patient care delivery.

• Resolve patient issues and complaints, identifying problems and finding appropriate solutions to ensure patient satisfaction while maintaining professional communication throughout the process.



The ideal candidate for this role at Ansible Health is a dynamic professional who thrives in a fast-paced, ever-changing startup environment. They should also possess exceptional English communication skills, both written and verbal, coupled with meticulous attention to detail and strong organizational abilities. They should be adept at managing multiple priorities, from patient onboarding to referral management.



Required Qualifications and Competencies

• Bachelor's degree in a relevant field, such as Nursing, Physical Therapy, or other healthcare-related fields.

• 2+ years of professional experience in a customer service or healthcare setting, demonstrating a deep understanding of patient care and care coordination.

• Solid grasp of the US healthcare system and its intricacies.

• Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills.

• Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.

• Proficiency with CRM systems, EMR software, and standard office applications (e.g., Google Suite)

• Training or experience in clinical medicine, such as a nursing degree, nursing assistant, or clinical scribe.

• Typing speed of at least 60 words per minute.

• Reliable internet connection (at least 30 Mbps) and a quiet home environment for work

• Ability to work in US time zones (EST)

• Achieve daily call quotas of 75-100 interactions.

• Adhere to established performance metrics to qualify for incentive plans tied to performance.



Nice to have

• Experience in the healthcare industry or with medical terminology.

• Proven track record in sales.

• Familiarity with remote patient monitoring systems.

• Knowledge of HIPAA regulations and healthcare privacy practices.



Job Details

Full-time: Full-time: 40 hours a week (with a possible shifting schedule including weekends)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work US hours - EST/EDT)

Key Responsibilities

title: Customer Support Representative
description:
Position Summary

This role suits someone who is tech-savvy, steady under pressure, and comfortable with flexible or shifting work schedules.

Required Skills

Preferred Qualifications

 – Quick to learn new tools and adapt to updated processes

Technical Setup Needed

 – Backup internet option (e.g., pocket Wi-Fi or secondary provider)

 – Backup power source (UPS or generator) to avoid service interruptions

 – Personal laptop or desktop computer

 – High-quality noise-cancelling headset

 – Quiet, distraction-free workspace



Why You’ll Love This Role



 Be part of a remote team where professionalism and clear communication are valued. You’ll receive structured scripts, ongoing training, and work in a no-cold-calling environment, allowing you to focus on providing exceptional service. This role offers the chance to develop your skills in customer interaction and service coordination while working in a supportive, improvement-focused setting.

title: Senior Bookkeeper / Virtual Assistant, Full-Time Remote
description:
We are a U.S.-based bookkeeping services company managing books for multiple clients across different industries. We need a senior bookkeeper who does not need to be managed.

That means you get assigned a client, you review their accounts, you understand their business, and you categorize their transactions correctly. On your own. The first time. If you need someone to tell you what each transaction means, this job is not for you.

We have two requirements that are not negotiable: integrity and independent judgment. In that order.

What This Job Actually Is

This is not a follow-the-instructions role. You will be given client accounts and expected to own them. That means:

Reviewing a client's chart of accounts and transaction history and understanding it without being coached

Categorizing transactions correctly based on the type of business they run

Spotting anything unusual, ambiguous, or wrong and flagging it before it becomes a problem

Knowing that a medical practice and a construction company have different books and applying that knowledge

Communicating problems early, not after the fact



If you are the type who waits to be told what to do, please do not apply. If you are the type who figures it out and escalates only the genuinely unclear stuff, we want to talk to you.

Day to Day Responsibilities

Transaction categorization across multiple client accounts, independently

Bank and credit card reconciliations

Accounts payable and accounts receivable

Payroll entry from Gusto payroll reports into QBO

Recording Stripe and other payment processor transactions, including fee handling

Financial reporting support

Sending templated client requests such as asking for bank statements. No freeform client communication.

Light VA work including entering financial data into Excel or Fathom for KPI reporting, and occasional website posting



Software

Required:

QuickBooks Online (QBO), must be proficient

Xero, must be proficient

Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets



Preferred, training available if needed:

Double, formerly known as Keeper, for transaction review and categorization

Fathom for KPI reporting



What We Are Looking For

Education:

BS Accountancy degree required. CPA preferred but not required.

Solid working knowledge of U.S. accounting standards and practices



Experience:

At least 3 years managing books for multiple U.S.-based clients at the same time

Proven ability to onboard a new client account without hand-holding

Experience across different business types and industries



Who you are:

Honest. You flag mistakes, including your own. You do not hide them.

Self-directed. You take ownership of your accounts.

Proactive. You raise issues before they become problems.

Available to overlap with U.S. Central Standard Time business hours

Strong written English



Pay and Benefits

$5.00 USD per hour, full-time at 40 hours per week, with CST overlap required.



Benefits include:

13th month pay

Paid Philippine public holidays

Paid trial period before full onboarding



We start every new hire with a paid trial period of 2 to 3 weeks on a single client account. It lets both sides confirm the fit before full onboarding. We are looking for someone long-term, not a short engagement.





How to Apply

Read every word of this section. Following instructions is part of the job.

Phase 1: Written Application

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph and include:

Your resume or work history with specific bookkeeping experience and the industries you have worked in

Your education and any accounting certifications

A short paragraph about why integrity matters to you in a bookkeeping role

Written answers to all four screening questions below



Screening Questions:

Question 1: You are assigned a new client who runs a physical therapy practice. You have their chart of accounts and three months of uncategorized transactions. Walk us through how you approach it. What do you look at first? How do you determine the right categories? And what do you do when a transaction is unclear?

Question 2: How do you record a Stripe payout in QuickBooks Online? Walk us through how you handle Stripe fees and the difference between the gross payout and the amount that actually hits the bank account.

Question 3: A client's Gusto payroll report shows gross wages of $4,200, employer taxes of $380, and net pay of $3,820. Walk us through every account you would use in QBO and how you confirm the net pay matches the bank withdrawal.

Question 4: Tell us about a time you caught a bookkeeping error, yours or someone else's. What was the error, what did you do, and what happened?

One more thing: somewhere in the middle of your application, include the word Tangerine exactly once in your subject line. Applications without it will not be reviewed.

Phase 2: Video Response

If your written application stands out, we will send you a client scenario and ask for a short Loom video, 2 to 3 minutes, walking through your answer. No preparation needed. This just confirms that what you wrote is what you actually know.

Phase 3: Live Interview

Finalists will have a short live video call with follow-up questions based on their earlier answers. It is a conversation, not a test. We want to make sure this works for both of us.





We run a professional operation, and we take the quality of our work seriously. If you are an accountant who owns their accounts, catches problems early, and does not need to be managed, we would like to hear from you.

We look forward to your application.

title: Remote Legal Assistant (Real Estate) | Illinois Residential Transactions | Hourly Contractor
description:
About the Role



The Minasian Law Firm is licensed in Illinois, New York, and Texas and is hiring a remote Legal Assistant to support residential real estate transactions (primarily Illinois) from contract to close. This is not a paralegal role. You will support the attorney and keep matters moving through disciplined coordination, documentation, follow-up, and deadline control.



What You Will Do

- Open and maintain matters in our case system (Smokeball)

- Coordinate with clients, agents, lenders, title companies, HOA, and opposing counsel

- Track key milestones and deadlines (attorney review, inspection, lender conditions, title issues, HOA docs, closing)

- Prepare drafts and transaction packets for attorney review (letters, checklists, standard forms, disclosures, addendums as directed)

- Organize documents, maintain file hygiene and naming conventions, and keep matter timelines up to date

- Send proactive follow-ups to prevent files from stalling

- Escalate issues with a clear summary and recommended next steps (not just questions)



Real Estate Knowledge Required (Must Have)

- Strong understanding of the residential real estate transaction flow (contract to close)

- Comfortable managing moving parts: inspection timeline, attorney review window, lender requests, HOA document retrieval, title commitment items, and closing coordination

- Able to identify what is missing and request it proactively

- Strong document and deadline discipline



What This Role Is Not

- This is not a paralegal role and does not involve giving legal advice, negotiating legal positions, or independently making legal determinations

- You will not sign legal documents on behalf of the firm



How We Work: Business Value Markers: At Minasian Law, how we work matters as much as what we deliver.



1) Protect Trust



- Double-check facts, documents, details, and fine print

- Maintain strict confidentiality and secure handling of client data

- Use security tools (MFA, secure file handling)

- Ask for review when unsure



2) Communicate Clearly and Often



- Every message is professional and purposeful, internally and externally

- Ask when unsure, make no assumptions

- Flag urgent items and update proactively

- Prevent stalls by keeping others in the loop



3) Make It Better



- Own outcomes, not just tasks

- Improve templates, checklists, and workflows

- Simplify and document what works



* Shorthand: Protect Trust. Communicate Clearly and Often. Make It Better.



Schedule



- Target: approximately +/- 20 hours per week (variable based on volume), including client-facing and operational work

- Must overlap with Chicago business hours (US Central Time) for coordination



Contractor Terms (Important)



- Hourly independent contractor

- Weekly timesheet submission (hours must be approved)

- Starting Rate: $7.50 USD/hour during probation period of 60 days

- Opportunity for rate increases as responsibilities grow and qualifications are demonstrated.

- Biweekly invoicing per our SOP

- Payment via Wise (or method designated by the firm)

- NDA must be signed before access or work begins



How to Apply:

Please send the following to 



- A brief cover letter outlining your U.S. residential real estate transaction support experience (what parts you can run end-to-end)

- Your resume with photo

- One example of how you have created or improved SOPs/workflows in past roles (brief description or link)

- Your availability (hours per week + time zone)

- Two professional references we can contact (name, role,
, phone, and how you worked together)

- Copies of any legal diplomas, certifications, or relevant training

- A link to your OnlineJobs.ph profile



Screening Questions (answer in your application)



- Transaction experience: Describe your experience supporting U.S. residential real estate transactions. Have you worked with contracts, title commitments, lender conditions, HOA docs, or closing coordination? Provide 2–3 examples.

- Outlook discipline: How do you manage a busy Outlook inbox and calendar so nothing is missed?

- Case management: Have you used Smokeball or another case/matter management system? Which one, and what did you use it for?

- SOP ownership: Give an example of a process or SOP you drafted or improved. What changed and what improved? Include the words blue fish in your answer.

- Reporting: How would you provide a short morning update (deadlines/priorities) and an end-of-day summary (accomplishments/blockers) to an attorney?

- Training: List any diplomas, certifications, or specialized training relevant to this role.



Important: Include your OnlineJobs.ph profile link in your application.

, calendar, and task flow.

These Business Value Markers are our day-to-day guideposts: clear actions that help us protect clients, collaborate effectively, and keep improving. They’re designed to be simple enough to remember, practical enough to use, and strong enough to uphold the reputation of the firm.



Protect Trust

- Build and refine systems so work is reliable

Protect Trust, Communicate Clearly & Often, Make It Better

Company: Staffing Services Company – Brooklyn, NY

Location: Remote

Employment Type: Full-Time / Part-Time

Work Hours: U.S. Eastern Time (EST)



Role Overview



We are seeking an experienced Remote Appointment Setter to support our sales team by identifying decision-makers, conducting outbound outreach, and setting qualified appointments. This role is ideal for a disciplined professional with a proven background in cold calling and online research for B2B services.



Required Experience (Must-Have)



Minimum 2 years of experience in cold calling for appointment setting (B2B preferred)



Minimum 2 years of experience researching and identifying decision-makers (HR, Operations, Owners, Hiring Managers) for products or services sold



Demonstrated ability to engage prospects professionally and confidently by phone and



Experience setting meetings for sales or business development teams



Key Responsibilities



Conduct high-volume outbound cold calls to U.S.-based businesses



Research companies and identify the correct decision-makers using LinkedIn, company websites, and online databases



Qualify prospects and schedule meetings for the sales team



Meet or exceed weekly and monthly appointment-setting targets



Additional Preferred Skills



Experience in staffing, recruiting, or B2B services



Strong spoken and written English communication skills



Ability to work independently in a remote environment



Comfortable working in U.S. time zones



Familiarity with CRM systems (HubSpot)



What We Offer



Competitive pay based on experience



Performance-based incentives



Long-term remote opportunity with a U.S.-based staffing company



Training and ongoing support



How to Apply:



Send the following to 
 



1. Please submit your resume and include:



2. Your years of experience in cold calling for appointment setting

title: Social Media Manager + Video Editor (Architecture + Real Estate Brand)

? Hours + Pay

You'll be the first voice our customers hear, the person keeping our crews on schedule, and the system-keeper that makes everything run smoothly.  your video and resume to

title: AI Content Operations Manager (SEO + Psychology Focus)
description:
About Big Life Journal:

We are a global brand dedicated to helping children develop a growth mindset. We don't just "write blogs"—we build a world-class knowledge base that AI engines (like Perplexity and ChatGPT) use to help parents and teachers.



The Role:

We are looking for a strategic AI Content Operations Manager to lead our long-form content and outreach. You aren't just a writer; you are a systems thinker who knows how to make our brand the #1 cited authority by AI.



Key Responsibilities:



AEO (Answer Engine Optimization): Researching "AI Gaps" where current AI models give weak answers about child resilience and creating the content to fill them.

AI-Human Hybrid Writing: Using our "Signature Gem" to draft 2,000-word deep dives, then "humanizing" them using tools like Phrasly/Junia to ensure they pass sentiment and quality checks.

Outreach Coordination: Identifying and contacting YouTube creators and parenting bloggers to coordinate mentions and collaborations.

Content Planning: Building a calendar that focuses on "Task-Based" queries (e.g., "What to say to a child who failed a test").



Requirements:



Expert-level prompting skills (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude).

Experience with "Humanizing" tools and sentiment analysis.

Ability to think like a psychologist/educator (high empathy).

Strong project management (
).



Preferred Qualifications:



Degree in Psychology, Early Childhood Education, or Social Work: You understand the "why" behind child behavior.

Pedagogical Literacy: You can explain complex concepts like "neuroplasticity" or "growth mindset" in a way that is simple for parents but scientifically accurate.

E-E-A-T Knowledge: You understand how to use your background to build brand authority in the eyes of AI and search engines.



?? MANDATORY TEST TASK (Submit with your application)

Please complete the following and
 it to 
 without this task will be ignored.



Part 1: The AI Visibility Plan



Identify the Gap: Use Perplexity or ChatGPT to find a common question about "children's confidence" or "growth mindset" that currently has a weak, generic, or unhelpful AI answer.



The Content Plan: Propose 3 blog titles that would "force" the AI to cite Big Life Journal as the primary expert source for that specific gap.



The Format: Explain how you would structure the page (e.g., would you use a comparison table, an "At a Glance" summary box, or an FAQ section?) to make it easy for AI to "scrape" and cite us.



Part 2: The "Humanizing" Sample



Ask an AI to write a 200-word intro for a blog about "Dealing with Math Anxiety."



Send me the original AI draft AND your edited/humanized version. Tell me which tools or specific editing techniques you used to make it sound like a "Big Life Journal" expert.

description:

title: Affiliate Program Team (SaaS Company) - Hiring 3 people

• Track commissions and payouts in Google Sheets

• Generate and verify referral links

• Maintain internal reporting dashboard

• Monitor affiliate activity and flag unusual activity



VA #2 – Affiliate Outreach & Recruitment

• Send outreach
s and LinkedIn messages using templates

• Send onboarding
s to new affiliates

s to inactive affiliates

• Comfortable using Google Sheets and basic online tools

Thank you.

title: Construction Estimator (Flooring Specialist)
description:
Job Summary



We are seeking a full-time, dedicated Construction Estimator (Flooring Specialist) to support a growing commercial flooring business. This role focuses on reviewing outsourced estimates, managing RFIs, and ensuring all plans, scope details, and final proposals are fully aligned before submission.



You will act as an internal quality control and coordination specialist, helping improve communication turnaround, reduce errors, and ensure accuracy across a high volume of commercial bids.



This role is ideal for someone with hands-on flooring estimating experience, strong attention to detail, and the ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.



Key Responsibilities



Review bids, takeoffs, and plans prepared by an outsourced estimation team



Manage the RFI (Request for Information) process:



Identify discrepancies, missing information, or scope conflicts in plans



Send RFIs to customers or project contacts



Follow up to ensure timely responses



Perform detailed quality checks to ensure the project scope aligns with plans and final proposals



Coordinate communication between the outsourced estimators and internal project managers



Respond to internal questions regarding plans, specifications, and scope details



Track clarifications, revisions, and updates to ensure nothing is missed



Handle a high volume of bid reviews with speed and accuracy



Requirements



Required: Proven experience in flooring estimation (commercial flooring preferred)



Strong understanding of:



Flooring materials, systems, and installation methods



Flooring terminology, specifications, and scope interpretation

title: Medical Sales Rep
description:
Job Title: Senior Remote Sales Consultant (Wellness & Longevity)

Company: Ivologist



Location: Remote (USA)



Experience: 5+ Years (Medical/SaaS/Telehealth Sales)



Comp Structure: Base + Uncapped Commission



The Opportunity



Ivologist is redefining the patient experience in medical weight loss (GLP-1), Men’s Health (TRT), and premium IV hydration. We are looking for a Remote Senior Sales Representative who thrives in a high-velocity, digital-first environment. With 49-state coverage. You will manage high-value leads  from your home office, acting as the face of our clinical excellence.



Key Responsibilities



Digital Lead Mastery: Manage a high volume of inbound inquiries via CRM , converting prospects into long-term wellness members.



Clinical Consulting: Use your 5+ years of expertise to explain complex protocols (Peptides, GLP-1s, and Micronutrients) to sophisticated clients.



Pipeline Management: Maintain a rigorous follow-up cadence and accurately forecast monthly revenue in a fast-paced remote setting.



Requirements



Experience: 5+ years in a high-stakes sales role. Experience in Telehealth, Med-Tech, or Boutique Wellness is a non-negotiable.



Remote Proficiency: Proven ability to hit quotas without in-person supervision. You must be a self-starter with a "hunter" mentality.



Tech Stack: Expert-level knowledge of CRM systems, Zoom/Teams, and asynchronous communication tools (Slack/Loom).



Clinical Fluency: Ability to quickly master the science behind IV infusions and metabolic health to provide expert-level consultations.



Setup: A professional, quiet home office environment with high-speed internet.



Application Requirement: The "3-Minute Discovery" Video



To be considered for this senior-level role, we require a brief video introduction (max 3 minutes). This is your chance to demonstrate the communication skills and clinical authority you’ve built over the last 5+ years.



Your Video Prompt:



The Intro: Briefly summarize your experience in medical/specialty sales.



The "Elevator Pitch": Imagine you are speaking to a high-net-worth client interested in a Medical Weight Loss (GLP-1) program. In 60 seconds, explain why Ivologist’s clinical oversight is superior to a generic online subscription.



The Tech Check: Ensure your lighting, audio, and background reflect a professional remote consulting environment.



How to Submit: Please upload your video to a hosting platform (Loom, YouTube Unlisted, or Google Drive) and include the link in your initial application or cover letter.

title: Customer Service Representative with Property Management Experience
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*



The Nabo Group is looking for 3 Customer Service Representatives with Property Management experience for our growing team, who enjoys solving problems, responsible, organized, detail oriented, and reliable to join our fun/hard working team. You must have excellent communication skills.



As a Customer Service Representative, you will play a critical role in fostering positive relationships with our clients and ensuring that their maintenance needs are met effectively and efficiently. Your work will directly impact customer satisfaction and help maintain our reputation for excellence in the maintenance industry.



Duties & Responsibilities:

- Client Communication: Act as the primary point of contact for clients, providing exceptional customer service and ensuring clear and professional communication. Respond to client inquiries via phone,
, and online platforms.

- Maintenance Coordination: Work closely with the maintenance team to schedule, track, and coordinate maintenance services, ensuring timely and accurate completion of tasks.

- Issue Resolution: Address and resolve client complaints or concerns regarding maintenance services in a timely, professional, and empathetic manner.

- Client Scheduling & Follow-Up: Schedule appointments for maintenance services and follow up with clients’ post-service to ensure satisfaction and gather feedback.

- Database Management: Maintain accurate and up-to-date records of client interactions, maintenance schedules, and work orders within the company’s CRM system.

- Problem Solving & Troubleshooting: Provide quick solutions to client issues and coordinate any necessary follow-up or additional service visits.

- Collaboration: Work closely with field technicians, supervisors, and other tea
mbers to ensure the smooth flow of maintenance services from start to finish.

- Service Knowledge: Stay informed about the company’s maintenance offerings and ensure clients are fully aware of the services available to them.

- Reporting & Documentation: Document client interactions, service requests, and resolutions. Generate reports to track the progress and outcomes of maintenance activities.

- Performs other duties that may be assigned from time to time.



Desired Qualifications:

- College degree or equivalent work experience.

- Experience with Real Estate is a plus.

- At least 6 months of experience working remotely.

- Excellent English communication skills (spoken and written).

- Excellent interpersonal skills, problem solving, and analytical skills.

- Strong customer service skills.

- Ability to multi-task and prioritize.

- Reliable and dependable.

- Excellent problem-solving skills.

- Ability to remain calm under pressure.

- Attention to details and highly organized.

- Great time management skills.

- Capable of keeping control of the call even when the customer is angry.

- Ethical and trustworthy.

- Great at relationship-building.

- Ability to work under minimal supervision.

- Friendly and fair.



Working conditions:

- Candidate must be able to work in US Time Zone

- Comfortable working with Hubstaff.

- The schedules are permanent and assigned by the department’s Manager. Schedules might change eventually depending on demand.

- Must have own work equipment that is not older than 2 years.

- Work equipment must be personal and not a family or shared computer/laptop.

- Internet speed must be no lower than 150 Mbps.

- Must have backup internet for emergencies (no lower than 100 Mbps) and a place to relocate during power or internet outages.



Compensation & Benefits:

- $6 USD per hour.

- PTO after 90 days of probationary period.

- HMO allowance after 90 days of probationary period.



How to apply:

1. Send an
 attachment with your updated resume in PDF format.

2. Please indicate that you're comfortable to start with $6 USD per hour.

3. Please send a link to your video recording introducing yourself & your relevant experience based on the job description.

4. Kindly send your application to 
 the subject line, kindly indicate the position Customer Service Representative with Property Management Experience - your name.



Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by
 for interview scheduling.

Looking forward to working with you!

title: Social Media Visual Content Producer| 8-Week Project | Two Luxury Wellness Brands
description:
We are hiring an organized, dependable visual content producer to create a complete set of social media assets for two luxury wellness brands.



All captions and content direction are already completed for the year. Your role is to produce the visuals: static Canva graphics and simple “Type 1” reels using templates and a consistent brand style.



Total scope: ~560 assets (static + simple reels)

Timeline: 8 weeks

Payment: Biweekly after deliverables are uploaded and reviewed (no upfront payment)



Brands



Magnolia Skin Therapy (MST) — luxury skincare / esthetics

Spa Haven Suites (SHS) — luxury wellness suites + salt therapy + infrared sauna



Style: clean, elevated, calm, consistent typography/spacing (not trendy/busy layouts).





Responsibilities



Phase 1 — Template Setup (Week 1)



* Create 6–10 reusable Canva templates per brand (12–20 total)

* Templates must support: education, quotes, promotions, services/amenities, announcements

* Apply consistent design rules (fonts, hierarchy, spacing, alignment)

* Submit templates for approval before bulk production begins



Phase 2 — Production (Weeks 2–8)



Using approved templates, you will:



* Produce finished static Canva graphics using provided captions/headlines

* Swap/crop imagery as needed while staying on-brand

* Create simple “Type 1” reels (Canva or CapCut):

  * text overlays

  * 3–6 clips

  * auto captions

  * basic music

* Export and organize all files by brand + week with clear naming



---



Delivery Method (Important)



All deliverables must be uploaded into our shared Google Drive using the folder structure provided.



Deliverables must include:



* Exported static graphics (format: PNG/JPG as specified)

* Exported reel files (MP4)

* Editable source files:



  * Canva templates and designs saved/packaged so we can edit later (method provided at start)

  * CapCut project files if used (if applicable)



All uploads must be organized by Brand ? Month/Week with consistent file naming.



---



Deliverables & Quotas



To keep this project on schedule:



Week 1:50 completed assets (static + reels mix) uploaded to Drive

Weeks 2–8: 140 completed assets every 2 weeks uploaded to Drive (or 70/week if preferred)



Payment is issued biweekly once the scheduled deliverable count is met and uploaded.

If quota is not met, payment is adjusted proportionally.



---



Compensation



* $4–$6/hour

* Estimated 25–30 hours/week during production

* Biweekly pay (no upfront pay)



---



Requirements



* Strong Canva skills (layout, alignment, typography hierarchy, consistency)

* Experience creating simple short-form reels in Canva or CapCut

* Fast, organized, deadline-driven

* Clear communication and dependable follow-through



---



To Apply (Required)



Please include:



1. 5 Canva posts from one brand you’ve designed (consistent set)

2. 1 example of a simple edited reel (not just a cover)

3. Confirmation you can meet the deliverable quotas and timeline

4. Your weekly availability (hours/week)

5. Your MBTI type (if known)

title: Bookkeeper (American Ledger)
description:
Part-Time Bookkeeper (Remote) – WLDM



White Light Digital Marketing (WLDM) is a league of e-commerce marketers working with top private equity firms in the US. We are not your typical agency – we are a team of industry leaders, and we expect the same from those who join us.



We are looking for a part-time Bookkeeper with strong experience in Australian tax compliance and a proven track record of managing financial operations. This role is remote and requires a minimum of 3 years of relevant experience.



Key Responsibilities



US Ledger

Daily bank reconciliation (~1,000 transactions/month)

Manage Mercury Bank, Wise, Crypto accounts (in setup), PayPal, and Payoneer

Process daily ad hoc invoicing

Process daily guest post payments (10–20 transactions on average)

Weekly cashflow updates and finance meetings

Weekly Accounts Receivable (AR) management

Monthly payroll for 70+ overseas subcontractors

Monthly client invoicing for retainers

Monthly financial reporting (Xero, Fathom)

Monthly finance meetings, including AP and AR reviews

Yearly tax return preparation

Cross-Ledger

Management of intercompany loan account



Qualifications



Minimum 3 years of bookkeeping/accounting experience

Strong knowledge of US tax compliance

Proficiency in Xero (experience with Fathom a plus)

Experience managing high-volume US transactions (including international accounts, crypto, and payment platforms)

Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple reporting cycles (weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly)

Strong communication skills with the ability to coordinate directly with stakeholders (including the CEO)



What We Offer

Flexible part-time remote position

Opportunity to work with a high-performing global team

Competitive pay based on experience

title: Linkedin Outbound Specialist + VA for Content Marketing Agency
description:
This is a part-time position with a dual role: Linkedin Outbound Management + General Virtual Assistant work. 



This will grow into full-time over time. 



I need an experienced Linkedin Lead gen specialist (no beginners please), to manage some aspects of my personal linkedin account to support business activity. This role will also be doing this for our clients over time.



This role will also be responsible for managing regular VA type tasks: invoicing, some project management, admin tasks, reporting, etc. 



Your Role -- Linkedin

 

- Building lists of targeted clients on Sales Nav

- Ensuring those contacts are valid and fit the client profile

- Sending 20-30 connect requests per day with customized messages (I'll provide scripts)

- Monitoring my personal profile activity and sending connect requests / personal DMs to some people who engage with our profile/content. There will be workflows to follow from this

- Reporting on the performance of different connect messages, connect rates, and outbound activity/results.

- Provide ideas for how to improve performance for both my account and clients.



Your Role -- VA



- Invoicing

- Reporting

- Project management in Clickup



Requirements: 

- Linkedin outbound experience

- 2 years of VA experience working for someone else. 



How to Apply.

1. Submit an application (DO NOT USE AI TO WRITE THIS)

2. Make the first word of your application "HOWDY" so I can see you read and follow instructions. 

3. Tell me about any relevant experience, including which platforms you have used to manage linkedin accounts



I am building out this role and there is some flexibility in it - if you have other ideas or experience I can use, I'd love to know it. 



ABOUT THE COMPANY

- We are a B2B content marketing company and consultancy.

- We're laid back, but place an extremely high value on quality and service to clients



Thanks! I look forward to meeting you!



Josh

title: Social Media & Content Virtual Assistant (15 hrs/week)
description:
About Us:

Magnolia Skin Therapy and Spa Haven Suites are wellness-focused businesses in Henderson, NV (Las Vegas area). We’re looking for a creative, proactive Social Media & Content VA to manage and grow our online presence across Instagram,
, and other channels.

Video Editing: Edit & schedule short-form videos (60–90s reels) with branded covers, overlays, captions, and music.



Podcast Clips: Repurpose highlights into social content.



Daily Engagement: Reply to comments/DMs within 24 hrs; interact with local spas, beauty influencers, and relevant hashtags (~30 min/day).

Grid Cohesion: Ensure IG grid and FB feeds are consistent with brand colors and aesthetics.



Requirements:



Strong experience with Instagram &
 management (samples required).



Canva & video editing skills (Reels/TikTok style preferred).



Familiarity with social SEO (hashtags, captions, keyword placement).



Organized and detail-oriented — able to follow content workflows.



What to Submit:

Resume

Portfolio + live sample posts/reels you created (not mockups)

MBTI Results

Short note on why you’d be a good fit

Answer this question: Describe a time you faced a challenge in creating or managing social media content. How did you handle it, and what was the outcome?



Compensation: $_300__/month (approx. 15 hrs/week), paid bi-weekly; based on experience. This role starts at 15 hours per week with a monthly rate. As the brands grow and campaigns expand, there will be potential to increase hours and responsibilities over time.

title: Medical VA / Onboarding Specialist
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/2475d4d7-7ed7-449e-a350-75eb5484b5db





What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

We are looking for a top-tier Patient Care Coordinator to become an integral part of our expanding team. This role is vital in delivering the best care possible to our patients, handling patient communications, and providing essential support to our clinical staff in their mission to offer exceptional care. The ideal candidate will demonstrate exemplary professionalism, excellent English language communication skills, and outstanding organizational abilities with meticulous attention to detail. This position offers an exciting opportunity to contribute significantly to our patient-centric approach in healthcare delivery.



Responsibilities

• Customize initial discussions based on patient needs, ensuring they understand how the program can address their specific health conditions, providing personalized benefits rather than general services.

• Manage inquiries through multiple communication channels (phone,
, etc.), including voi
 inbox management and message routing, providing prompt and accurate responses.

• Process and manage referrals, working towards efficient conversion within a 7-10 day timeframe while managing multiple referral loop processes for a large patient panel.

• Perform comprehensive data entry including clinical survey scores/results, diagnostic tests, remote physiologic monitoring (RPM), and remote therapeutic monitoring (RTM) data streams.

• Schedule and coordinate patient appointments while maintaining communication with patients regarding their care schedule.

• Manage and process inbound and outbound medical records, ensuring proper documentation and organization.

• Maintain accurate EMR records by updating care notes, face sheets, and ensuring the integrity of patient information.

• Attend clinical meetings, take detailed notes, and follow up on action items and project timelines.

• Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of physicians, engineers, designers, and care tea
mbers to continuously improve patient care delivery.

• Resolve patient issues and complaints, identifying problems and finding appropriate solutions to ensure patient satisfaction while maintaining professional communication throughout the process.



The ideal candidate for this role at Ansible Health is a dynamic professional who thrives in a fast-paced, ever-changing startup environment. They should also possess exceptional English communication skills, both written and verbal, coupled with meticulous attention to detail and strong organizational abilities. They should be adept at managing multiple priorities, from patient onboarding to referral management.



Required Qualifications and Competencies

• Bachelor's degree in a relevant field, such as Nursing, Physical Therapy, or other healthcare-related fields.

• 2+ years of professional experience in a customer service or healthcare setting, demonstrating a deep understanding of patient care and care coordination.

• Solid grasp of the US healthcare system and its intricacies.

• Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills.

• Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.

• Proficiency with CRM systems, EMR software, and standard office applications (e.g., Google Suite)

• Training or experience in clinical medicine, such as a nursing degree, nursing assistant, or clinical scribe.

• Typing speed of at least 60 words per minute.

• Reliable internet connection (at least 30 Mbps) and a quiet home environment for work

• Ability to work in US time zones (EST)

• Achieve daily call quotas of 75-100 interactions.

• Adhere to established performance metrics to qualify for incentive plans tied to performance.



Nice to have

• Experience in the healthcare industry or with medical terminology.

• Proven track record in sales.

• Familiarity with remote patient monitoring systems.

• Knowledge of HIPAA regulations and healthcare privacy practices.



Job Details

Full-time: Full-time: 40 hours a week (with a possible shifting schedule including weekends)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage

Location: Remote (offshore, and willing to work US hours - EST/EDT)

title: Healthcare Technical Support
description:
To be considered, you must fill out the application form below:


/ansiblehealth/17b2a6df-a72d-480e-8c91-daa3830ac000



What is Ansible Health?

Ansible Health unites physicians, nurses, respiratory therapists, and tech experts from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Google, Amazon, and other top institutions to revolutionize care for chronic disease patients. We combine elite clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology to deliver personalized, efficient care at scale. Our team's groundbreaking research, published in leading journals like Nature and PLOS, demonstrates our commitment to innovation. Backed by Bessemer, First Round Capital, AlleyCorp, Breyer Capital, and Inflect Capital, we're a Series A startup partnering with leading healthcare systems nationwide. Our mission is to genuinely improve patient outcomes, not just optimize metrics. If you're passionate about transforming healthcare through technology, join our diverse team in making a real impact.



The Job at a Glance

As a Technical Product Support Specialist, your primary responsibility is to provide patients with exceptional technical support for our products and services. You will daily engage non-tech-savvy patients on technical phone calls. Most of our patients are older adults located in the USA. These calls involve guiding patients through using their Android tablet and verifying it in the backend through Samsung Knox, ensuring that RPM devices are connected and transmitting accurate data, and assisting them in joining their appointments through our tablet via Zoom. Occasionally, patients may require additional help during their time in our program. In some instances, verifying data through Azure (Device Analytics and CosmosDB) may be necessary.



Furthermore, you will provide technical support to our clinical team, and ensure they are prepared for their virtual appointments. They may also require assistance using Athenahealth, such as modifying visits and checking in/out. You will handle general general technical support duties, which demand a solid understanding of our tech stack and products (i.e., Android Tablet, Zoo
etings and phone, Google Workspace). You should be capable of providing basic troubleshooting steps and assistance to remove obstacles for clinicians/support staff in their work, and are expected to be able to have on-call availability for urgent situations where patient care is affected.



Collaborating closely with our Engineering Team is also an essential aspect of your role to effectively escalate issues requiring broader coordination and resolution. GitLab and Azure DevOps are the primary ticketing systems used for filing issues and requests. Continuous follow-ups and monitoring are expected until the issue is resolved end-to-end. In addition, you are expected to be able to present and take notes on meetings about issues encountered in Tech.



The ideal candidate should be comfortable navigating ambiguity due to the ever-evolving nature of our startup. To excel in this role, excellent problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills are essential.



A Technical Product Support Specialist is expected to:

• Maintain a reliable, high-speed internet connection

• Handle Tech Support requests filed by clinicians or patients from end to end

• Manage and organize various project tools (e.g., GitLab, AzureDevOps, spreadsheets, bug trackers, documents)

• Conduct Tech Calls for non-tech-savvy patients (mainly older adults) in the USA

• Guide patients through Android tablet usage and confirm settings through Samsung Knox

• Work with RPM devices to ensure they are connected and transmitting accurate data

• Collaborate with Engineering Team and Program Managers to escalate and resolve issues

• Assist clinicians and company support staff with technical issues

• Run queries and check analytics using Azure’s CosmosDB or Device Analytics

• Become extremely familiar with our proprietary app, tablets, RPM devices, and backend systems.

• Support clinical team with equipment setup and checking applied security policies

• Present and take notes on Technical meetings, specifically on issues encountered and plans to resolve them.



Qualifications and Experience

• Prior experience in a customer support role, such as Technical Support Specialist or Support Engineer

• Expertise in Android Tablets, Samsung Knox Suite, and Knox Manage

• Proficiency in managing Zoo
etings and Zoom Phone

• Familiarity with medical devices (e.g., Blood Pressure monitor, Pulse Oximeter)

• Knowledge of Athenahealth preferred

• Experience using issue tracking software and remote support tools (e.g., Gitlab, Azure DevOps) is a plus

• Understanding of technology systems, particularly Google Workspace

• Positive, enthusiastic attitude and excellent English language skills (C2 level)

• Skilled in technical debugging techniques (e.g., analyzing metrics and logs on Azure)

• Strong written and verbal communication, problem-solving skills, and detail-oriented

• Ability to manage multiple priorities effectively

• Healthcare setting experience advantageous



Type of Role

Full-time: 40 hours a week (Monday - Friday; 9pm - 6pm EST/EDT)

Benefits: Flexible PTO and HMO coverage.

Level: Mid-Career, 3-5 years relevant work experience

Location: Remote

title: Senior Video Editor & Motion Graphics Specialist
description:
Overview

We are looking for a highly skilled, expert-level video editor with advanced proficiency in visual effects, motion graphics, and post-production. This is not an entry-level or intermediate role. We need someone who can take raw content we provide and deliver polished, professional, high-impact video with effects, transitions, color grading, and sound design that meets broadcast and digital publishing standards.

You will receive all content (footage, audio, images, creative direction) and are expected to deliver completed edits with expert-level effects and finishing—on time and on brief.

Responsibilities

• Edit video content from raw footage to final delivery, including music videos, promotional content, social media clips, and branded video

• Apply advanced visual effects, motion graphics, transitions, and compositing to elevate production value

• Perform professional color grading and correction to ensure visual consistency and cinematic quality

• Handle audio mixing, sound design, and syncing to ensure clean, balanced final audio

• Deliver multiple format exports optimized for specific platforms (Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, broadcast, etc.)

• Interpret creative briefs and deliver work that matches the intended vision without requiring excessive revisions

• Manage project files with organized naming conventions and deliver assets in a clean, structured format

• Meet deadlines consistently and communicate proactively about timelines and any production concerns

Required Skills & Qualifications

• Expert-level proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, and/or DaVinci Resolve

• Advanced motion graphics and visual effects capabilities (kinetic typography, particle effects, compositing, tracking, keying)

• Strong color grading skills with a trained eye for cinematic and commercial aesthetics

• Proficiency in audio editing and basic sound design within video editing software

• Deep understanding of frame rates, codecs, export settings, and platform-specific delivery requirements

• Minimum 3–5 years of professional video editing experience with a portfolio that demonstrates expert-level work

• Ability to work independently with minimal direction once a creative brief is provided

Preferred (Not Required)

• Experience editing music videos and artist promotional content

• Familiarity with Cinema 4D, Blender, or other 3D tools for integrated effects

• Experience with animated thumbnails, YouTube packaging, or social media content design

• Understanding of current social media video trends and platform algorithms

What We Provide

• All raw footage, audio files, images, and supporting assets

• Creative direction, briefs, and reference materials for each project

• Clear deadlines and structured feedback process

• A professional, respectful working relationship with timely communication

title: Construction Cold Caller
description:
Construction Cold Caller | Appointment Setter Roofing and Energy Programs



Type of Work: Full Time

Hourly Rate: $6 per hour + commissions

Hours per Week: 40

Work Schedule: U.S. Eastern Time Zone



Job Overview



We are hiring experienced Construction Cold Callers and Appointment Setters to connect homeowners with roofing solutions and energy efficiency programs. This role focuses on booking qualified appointments for homeowners interested in roof upgrades and rebate-based energy improvement programs designed to lower household costs.



This position is ideal for candidates with a background in construction, roofing, solar, or energy efficiency appointment setting who can confidently explain program benefits and convert conversations into booked meetings.



Key Responsibilities



• Cold call homeowners and introduce roofing and energy efficiency solutions

• Educate homeowners on rebate programs and cost-saving benefits

• Handle objections and build trust quickly

• Book qualified appointments for the sales team

• Maintain accurate call notes and appointment records



Required Qualifications



• Proven experience in appointment setting or phone sales

• Background in construction, roofing, solar, or energy efficiency preferred

• Strong English communication skills

• Spanish speaking is a plus but not required

• Confident handling homeowner objections

• Reliable, organized, and results driven

• Ability to work full time in U.S. EST or CST hours



Compensation and Growth



• $6 per hour base pay

• High commission incentives for consistent performance

• Long-term opportunity for top performers

• Stable workload with growth potential



This Role Is Not a Fit If



• You have no experience in construction, roofing, or energy programs

• You have never booked qualified sales appointments

• You cannot work U.S. time zones

• You are seeking short-term or temporary work



Why Join Our Team



• Competitive hourly pay plus commissions

• Supportive professional team

• Long-term, secure position

• Opportunity to help homeowners reduce costs and improve their homes



How to Apply



To be considered, follow all steps exactly.



Record a short voice introduction using Vocaroo explaining your relevant experience.



Upload your resume to Google Drive and ensure link access is set to anyone can view.



Send both the Vocaroo link and Google Drive resume link in your application.



At the very top of your message, write one sentence explaining why you are the best candidate for this role.



Use the exact subject line: Blorange Walrus



Applications missing any of the above steps will not be reviewed.

title: Customer service
description:
Customer Service Representative – Women's Fashion | Remote | Long-Term

Full-Time | $3.50/hr | Paid Weekly | Remote



We run a women's fashion dropshipping business and we're looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Customer Service Representative to join our team for the long haul. We have clear systems, SOPs, and templates in place — we just need the right person to execute them consistently and professionally.

If you love helping people, take pride in clean communication, and want stable long-term work with a team that supports your growth, read on.



What You'll Be Doing



Responding to customer
s regarding orders, tracking, refunds, replacements, and sizing — within a 24-hour window

Following our pre-written templates and SOPs precisely and consistently

Managing and resolving customer complaints in a polite, professional manner

Logging refunds, complaints, and replacements accurately in Google Sheets

Generating weekly reports per store

Flagging recurring issues to the team lead

Contributing to improving our support documentation over time





What We're Looking For

Google Sheets (reporting and logging)


 (team communication)





Someone who is organized, reliable, and consistent — we have zero tolerance for duplicate
s, vague answers, or off-topic replies

Available full-time (40 hrs/week) once onboarded

Not currently holding another full-time position

Available to start immediately





What We Offer



$3.50/hour, paid weekly

Full-time, long-term role (starting with a 2-week paid trial)

Video training, SOPs,
 templates, and worksheets provided

Direct access to a team lead throughout onboarding
