title: Prop Firm Trader / Day Trader (FX/Crypto/Futures)
description:
Hello Traders!



Isla Capital is expanding, and we are looking for disciplined, experienced prop firm traders and day traders to join our growing team. We are seeking professionals who can trade evaluation and funded accounts with established prop firms such as FTMO, Apex, Alpha Capital Group, or similar companies.



This opportunity is for traders who understand prop firm rules, practice strong risk management, and can trade consistently. Over time, this role will grow into live portfolio management.



Responsibilities:

• Trade prop firm evaluation and funded accounts

• Follow all prop firm rules and drawdown limits strictly

• Maintain daily trading journals

• Provide regular performance reports



Requirements and Qualifications:

• Proven experience trading prop firm accounts

• Strong discipline and risk management skills

This is a performance-driven opportunity for serious traders who want to grow long-term with a professional trading operation. Our mission is to help Filipino traders earn meaningful income for themselves and their families.

title: Virtual Case Manager — Personal Injury
description:
We are seeking an experienced, highly motivated Virtual Case Manager to join our Personal Injury Division remotely from the Philippines. This is a full-time position supporting our personal injury attorneys and clients throughout the lifecycle of each case.



The ideal candidate has direct experience working with a Texas personal injury law firm, possesses excellent English communication skills with no accent or language barrier, and is comfortable interacting with American clients, attorneys, medical providers, and insurance adjusters by phone, video, and
.



MUST: You must reply with 1) a cover letter or message highlighting your experience 2) a clear video where I can see and hear why you are a good candidate, 3) your resume. If you skip any of these, your application will be ignored. 



Responsibilities

Case Management & Administration

• Manage a caseload of active personal injury cases from intake through resolution

• Maintain and update case management software with accurate, current case information

• Organize and maintain digital case files, including medical records, correspondence, billing statements, and legal documents

• Track critical deadlines, statutes of limitations, and calendar key dates

• Prepare demand packages, settlement summaries, and supporting documentation



Client Communication

• Serve as a primary point of contact for clients, providing regular case status updates

• Conduct phone and video calls with clients in a professional, empathetic, and clearly understandable manner

• Respond promptly to client inquiries and ensure a high level of client satisfaction

• Coordinate medical appointments, treatment plans, and follow-ups with clients



Medical Records & Treatment Coordination

• Request, obtain, organize, and review medical records and bills from providers

• Follow up with medical providers on outstanding records and treatment updates

• Track client treatment progress and ensure gaps in treatment are addressed proactively

• Prepare medical chronologies and summaries for attorney review



Insurance & Third-Party Communication

• Communicate with insurance adjusters regarding claims, liens, and settlement negotiations

• Submit and follow up on insurance claims, property damage claims, and PIP/MedPay applications

• Obtain and review insurance policy information, including coverage limits and declarations pages



Legal Support

• Assist attorneys with case evaluation, strategy preparation, and litigation support

• Draft correspondence, letters of representation, and lien resolution communications

• Coordinate with referral attorneys, co-counsel, and expert witnesses as needed

• Ensure compliance with Texas personal injury laws, procedures, and ethical requirements



Requirements

Required Qualifications

• Minimum 2 years of direct experience as a case manager or legal assistant at a Texas personal injury law firm

• Demonstrated knowledge of Texas personal injury case processes, including pre-litigation and litigation workflows

• Fluent, clear, and professional English communication — both written and verbal — with no significant accent or language barrier when speaking with American clients and colleagues

• Experience using case management software (e.g., Filevine, Litify, CASEpeer, SmartAdvocate, or similar platforms)

• Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, Google Workspace, and standard communication tools (Zoom, Slack, Loom, etc.)

• Reliable high-speed internet connection and a professional, quiet home office setup with appropriate lighting for video calls

• Availability to work during U.S. Central Time business hours (8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST)



Preferred Qualifications

• Experience with demand preparation, medical record summarization, and lien negotiation

• Familiarity with Texas insurance laws, PIP/MedPay, and UM/UIM claims

• Knowledge of medical terminology and ability to review and interpret medical records

• Prior experience working remotely for a U.S.-based law firm



How to Apply

To be considered for this position, you must submit all three of the following:



1. Loom Video Introduction — Record a 2–3 minute Loom video introducing yourself. Tell us about your experience with Texas personal injury cases, why you’re interested in this role, and walk us through a typical case you managed. This video is essential for us to evaluate your communication skills, professionalism, and English fluency.



2. Cover Letter — A tailored cover letter explaining your relevant experience with Texas personal injury law, the case management tools you’ve used, and what makes you a strong fit for this position.



3. Resume/CV — An updated resume highlighting your personal injury case management experience, technical skills, and professional references.



Applications that do not include all three items will not be considered.

title: Operations Lead
description:
We’re hiring our first Operations Lead—a strategic doer who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and build from the ground up. In the early stages, this role will be hands-on: managing day-to-day operations, creating SOPs, supporting client delivery, and refining internal processes. As the business scales, this person will transition into a leadership role, building and managing a remote team of CSRs, Project Managers, and Admins to support our growing client base.



This is a rare opportunity to shape the operational backbone of a healthcare startup from day one.



Key Responsibilities:

Program Execution & Client Support

- Oversee the delivery of dietary and exercise counseling programs to partner clinics

- Serve as the primary point of contact for medical clients, ensuring smooth onboarding, service delivery, and ongoing support

- Translate clinical goals into operational workflows that are scalable and patient-centered



SOP Development & Process Building:

- Create and refine SOPs for service delivery, client onboarding, reporting, and internal coordination

- Identify gaps and inefficiencies, and implement improvements that support quality, compliance, and scalability

- Document best practices and build operational playbooks for future hires



Wearing Multiple Hats:

- Contribute to marketing strategy and planning, including the creation of high-quality marketing materials

- Build internal processes from zero, laying the groundwork for scalable operations across departments

- Collaborate cross-functionally to support evolving business needs beyond core operations



Team Building & Leadership (Future Phase):

- Once business demand increases, recruit and onboard remote support staff (CSRs, Admins, Project Managers)

- Train and manage the team to uphold service standards and client satisfaction

- Foster a culture of accountability, empathy, and continuous improvement



Operational Oversight:

- Create and monitor KPIs such as client satisfaction, task completion, turnaround time, and engagement metrics

- Ensure compliance with healthcare standards and client-specific requirements

- Support billing, scheduling, and reporting functions as needed.



Qualifications

- 2+ years of experience in leadership, operations, client success, or program management

- Proven ability to build systems from scratch and thrive in a startup environment

- Strong communication skills - verbal and written

- Highly organized, proactive, and adaptable to shifting priorities

- Experience managing remote teams or cross-functional projects is a plus

title: Virtual Operations Assistant | Prime
description:
Please read the job description all the way to the bottom. Instructions on how to proceed are included there.



Virtual Operations Assistant

Rate: 6-7/hr (USD) 

Location: Remote

Practices Supported: Prime Heart and Vascular 

Reporting To: Practice Manager



 About Prime Heart and Vascular

Prime Heart and Vascular is a fast-growing, physician-led cardiology practice based in Plano, Texas, committed to delivering comprehensive and compassionate care. As a small, high-touch clinic, every tea
mber plays a vital role in our success. We’re now seeking a versatile and proactive Virtual Operations Assistant to support two practices and help drive daily operational excellence.



 The Opportunity

This remote role is ideal for a highly organized, solutions-driven professional who thrives in a fast-paced healthcare environment. You’ll support core administrative functions, coordinate logistics, and step into various roles as needed to ensure smooth operations across both practices. If you’re flexible, detail-oriented, and ready to make a meaningful impact from behind the scenes, we’d love to connect.



 Key Responsibilities

Operational Oversight & Staff Support

Proactively assist clinical and administrative staff to ensure daily tasks are completed and patient flow remains smooth

Track and report key performance indicators (KPIs) such as charting compliance, appointment accuracy, and throughput

Monitor adherence to clinic policies, procedures, and HIPAA regulations

Serve as the first point of contact for operational challenges, offering calm and effective solutions

Virtual Logistics & Resource Coordination

Manage weekly and monthly staff schedules, time-off requests, and coverage planning

Oversee supply ordering and inventory tracking remotely, coordinating with on-site staff to prevent shortages and control costs

Liaise with external vendors (e.g., IT support, equipment repair) to ensure timely service and minimal disruption

Cross-Functional Coverage

Step in to support other clinic roles as needed, including front desk, billing, scheduling, or patient coordination

Provide backup support during staff absences or peak workload periods

Maintain flexibility and readiness to adapt to shifting priorities across both practices

Administrative & Communication Support

Provide virtual backup for front and back-office functions, including:

Answering phones via VoIP

Scheduling appointments

Verifying insurance

Assisting with non-clinical patient coordination

Maintain secure digital filing systems for administrative, personnel, and operational records

Draft and manage professional correspondence, reports, and communications for the Practice Manager and physician team

 

Qualifications & Traits

Required Experience

2+ years in an administrative, coordinator, or operations support role within a medical or specialty practice (Cardiology experience is a plus)

Proven ability to manage scheduling, inventory, and team coordination remotely

Experience tracking performance metrics and supporting accountability across teams

Proficiency in EMR/EHR systems, medical office software, and Microsoft Office Suite

Mindset & Attributes

Ownership Mentality: You treat the clinic’s success as your own and anticipate needs before they arise

Organized & Detail-Oriented: You thrive on precision and process, especially in clinical settings

Reliable & Consistent: You’re the dependable backbone of operations, trusted to keep things running smoothly

Professional Communicator: You interact calmly and clearly with staff, physicians, vendors, and patients

Flexible & Versatile: You’re ready to wear multiple hats and jump in wherever support is needed

Cost-Conscious: You understand the value of lean operations and deliver impact without excess overhead



If you are interested in moving forward, please record a short Loom video (3–5 minutes) highlighting how your experience aligns with the key responsibilities in the attached job description.



Then
 the following to 

1. Your updated resume

2. Your Loom video link

3. Subject: Virtual Operations Assistant | Prime

title: Controller (CPA)
description:
Company Overview:



Altametrics



Trusted by major players throughout the industry, we have been a leading provider of SaaS solutions to businesses since 1997. The company's philosophy is to combine software design with comprehensive consultancy and support services to deliver business-critical applications.



Our enterprise software solutions deliver ROI by helping businesses increase profitability, improve customer service and value, integrate with other business applications, and readily access information to make informed decisions.



We offer a comprehensive product line of enterprise software and professional services addressing the needs of various departments and executive management. Our applications are ideal for executives who require real-time access to enterprise data. The software solutions are web-based, designed for quick deployment, scalable for growth, reliable, and readily available.



Key Responsibilities:

Financial Reporting & Close: Owns the monthly, quarterly, and annual close process, delivering accurate financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) in compliance with US GAAP.

Technical Accounting: Researches and resolves complex accounting issues, including revenue recognition, intercompany transactions, and multi-entity consolidation.

Team Leadership & Review: Supervises and mentors accounting staff, delegating work and reviewing reconciliations, AP/AR, and payroll to ensure quality and accuracy.

Internal Controls & Audit: Establishes, monitors, and improves internal controls, and acts as the primary contact for external US audits.

Strategic Planning: Develops budgets, forecasts, and cash flow projections, and analyzes variances to support executive decision-making.

Process Improvement: Optimizes the accounting technology stack (e.g., QuickBooks Online, Xero, 
) to drive efficiency and automation.

Compliance: Ensures compliance with US federal, state, and local tax requirements.



Requirements & Qualifications:

License: Certified Public Accountant (CPA), with strong knowledge of US GAAP preferred.

Experience: 5+ years of progressive accounting experience, with at least 2–3 years in a senior or controller-level role, preferably in a US-based company or BPO setting.

Technical Skills: High proficiency in QuickBooks Online or Xero, and advanced Microsoft Excel (modeling, lookups, pivot tables).

Communication: Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal) for presenting to US management.

Work Schedule: Willingness to work US business hours (night shift in the Philippines)



**CPA LICENSE REQUIRED**

**PERMANENT REMOTE (MUST HAVE OWN EQUIPMENT)**

**13-18 USD PER HOUR FIXED (Paid bi-weekly via Payoneer/Paypal)**

**40 HOURS PER WEEK (Weekends Off)**

**PACIFIC STANDARD TIME (Night Shift)**

**MONITASK TIME TRACKER**

title: Digital Ads Platform Pixel Tracking Specialist – Pixels, Events & Attribution
description:
We’re Not Hiring a Tag Installer. We’re Hiring a Signal Guardian.

(PLEASE DO NOT REPLY DIRECTLY TO THIS POST. IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO APPLY, FOLLOW THE STEPS AT THE END.)



Pay: Negotiable flat rate per 2-week period (USD), based on experience

Pay Timing: Paid once every 2 weeks. 80 hours per period, agreed upon at hire

Location: Remote (Philippines-based applicants only)

Role Type: Full-time | Long-term

Team Scope: Owns tracking integrity across accounts and platforms



Why This Role Exists

Ads don’t fail when creative is bad.

They fail when signals lie.



At Black Hat Strategy, we operate in restricted and volatile advertising environments where performance decisions are only as good as the data behind them. When tracking breaks, buyers react to ghosts. Budgets get pulled. Escalations happen for the wrong reasons.



We’re hiring a Tracking Specialist to ensure that what we see is real, what fires is accurate, and what we optimize against can be trusted.



This role exists so:

Media Buyers aren’t guessing

Escalation teams aren’t chasing false alarms

Leadership isn’t making decisions on bad data

This is not about “installing a pixel once.”

This is about ongoing signal integrity.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing

You won’t be running ads.

You’ll be protecting the data behind them.



As a Tracking Specialist, you will:

Set up and validate tracking across ecommerce platforms

 (Shopify, custom sites, and other CMS environments)

Configure and audit:

Meta Pixel

Conversion API (CAPI)

Event prioritization & AEM

Deduplication logic



Use tools like:

Meta Events Manager

Google Tag Manager

Platform diagnostics

Verify that events fire correctly across:

Browsers

Devices

Consent states

Document setups, issues, and fixes clearly inside ClickUp

Support Escalation & Recovery by validating whether issues are technical or behavioral



You are not reacting to noise.

You are confirming truth.



What This Role Owns (From the SOP)

Tracking setup and validation

Ongoing signal health monitoring

Technical audits during escalations

Confirmation that performance drops are real

Documentation of tracking logic and changes



If tracking is wrong, this role finds it.

If tracking is right, this role proves it.



This Role Is for Someone Who…

Has hands-on experience with:

Meta Pixel & Events Manager

CAPI

Google Tag Manager

Has a web development or technical background

 (HTML, JavaScript, data layers, CMS platforms)

Understands how tracking behaves across browsers and devices

Thinks in root causes, not surface symptoms

Documents work cleanly and precisely

Enjoys deep, technical problem-solving

This role rewards people who value accuracy while maintaining speed.



This Role Is Not for Someone Who…

Only knows “copy/paste” pixel setups

Avoids technical debugging

Gets frustrated digging through logs or diagnostics

Wants creative or strategic ownership

Dislikes documentation



How You’ll Work With the Team

Media Buyers execute campaigns… you ensure their signals are real

Escalation & Recovery Lead investigates issues… you validate the data layer

Project Manager ensures flow… you keep tracking work visible and documented

Ops Head sets standards… you enforce technical integrity



You are not creating performance.

You are protecting it.



Why You’ll Like Working Here

Deep technical ownership

Respect for careful, precise work

Clear systems and SOPs

No guesswork, no chaos

Long-term role with real importance



We don’t reward speed for speed’s sake.

We reward correctness.



Application Process

PLEASE DO NOT JUST REPLY TO THIS POST ON THE JOB BOARD.

To be considered, you must complete ALL STEPS BELOW.

The Google Form is your Online Interview.



Step 1: Take a short online IQ test

We respect intelligence and we think you probably do too.

Go to the Fast Learner Free IQ Test and complete it

Create a free account at the end

Save the results URL for Step 3

?? Applicants without a valid link will not be considered



Step 2: Record a 2–3 minute Loom video (camera ON)

Create a free account at Loom

Speak clearly in English

Tell us:

Your paid media experience

How you think about optimization and ROAS

How you’ve helped others improve performance

Save the Loom URL for Step 3



Step 3: Complete the Online Interview

NO AI ALLOWED. We want to hear you.

Open the Application Form

Enter your contact details

Paste:

IQ test results link

Loom video link

Answer all questions honestly and thoughtfully

Your answers matter more than perfect formatting.



Step 4: Live Video Interview (camera on)

This is where we get to know how you think.

I personally review every candidate who completes the steps above and will reach out directly to those who feel like the right fit.



If this role speaks to you, good.

That means you’re probably the kind of person we’re looking for.

Bring your best.

We’ll bring the system.



Link to Online Interview Form:

title: Texas Real Estate VA
description:
REAL ESTATE TRANSACTION COORDINATOR ASSISTANT (FULL-TIME | 8 AM – 5 PM CST)

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Company: Equity Real Estate – Top-Producing Houston Team

PLEASE READ BEFORE YOU APPLY, and make sure to follow all instructions carefully. Anyone who fails to follow them will be

skipped automatically.



WORK SCHEDULE

• Full-time: Monday to Friday, 8 AM – 5 PM CST

• Includes 1-hour unpaid break

• Long-term position with growth and performance bonuses



ROLE SUMMARY

You’ll support our in-house Transaction Coordinator and Broker in managing all active residential resale and lease

transactions. This position requires strong knowledge of Texas real estate processes, TREC forms, and excellent

communication skills to coordinate between clients, title companies, and vendors.

When the primary Transaction Coordinator is unavailable, you’ll step in to ensure that pending transactions continue

smoothly through closing.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Transaction Coordination Support (Resale & Lease)

• Assist the Transaction Coordinator in managing all pending deals from contract to close

• Track important deadlines (option period, appraisal, financing, and closing dates)

• Prepare, review, and upload TREC forms and supporting documents

• Communicate with title companies, lenders, inspectors, and clients to maintain transaction flow

• Schedule inspections, repairs, and appraisals as needed

• Monitor and update online tracking database to ensure all deals are up to date



Lease Management & Communication

• Handle inquiries for rental listings, from initial inquiry to signed lease contract

• Prepare lease agreements and renewals for landlord and tenant signatures

• Communicate directly with landlords, tenants, and agents to coordinate showings and lease steps



Administrative & Phone Support

• Answer and route incoming office calls in a professional, friendly manner

• Respond to
 and CR
ssages promptly and accurately

• Assist in maintaining digital files, checklists, and CRM updates



TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW

HAR / MLS

Follow Up Boss

Qualia

Google Workspace

Zipform / TransacDesk

WhatsApp

Zoom



QUALIFICATIONS

• 2+ years experience as a Real Estate Transaction or Lease Coordinator (Texas experience strongly preferred)

• Working knowledge of TREC contracts and Texas closing timelines

• Experience handling lease inquiries and agreements

• Excellent English communication – written and spoken

• Clear, confident phone voice with little to no accent

• Highly organized, dependable, and detail-oriented

• Stable internet connection & quiet home workspace



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

• $7 per hour (USD)

• Performance bonuses after 6 months

• Annual review for pay increase and career advancement

• Work directly with a Top-Producing Houston Realtor® & Associate Broker

• Supportive team and clear systems to help you succeed



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING WITH US

• Structured systems and supportive culture

• Hands-on mentorship from our in-house Transaction Coordinator and Broker

• Consistent workflow with clear training and advancement opportunities

• Long-term role with potential to grow into Lead Transaction Coordinator



TO APPLY

**Send your resume, a 1-minute voice introduction, and a short note describing your Texas real estate (particularly in TAR

forms) or MLS transaction experience.

Subject line: Application – Transaction Coordinator Assistant | [Your Name]



Please make sure to follow all instructions carefully. Anyone who fails to follow them will be skipped automatically.

title: Research Real Estate and Foreclosure research night time position
description:
Research-Forward (Detail-Oriented VAs)



Real Estate Research Specialist & Appointment Setter (Remote)



This is a nighttime positition so you would be working based on Eastern time zone in the United States  



Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)

Hours: Flexible, Part-time to Full-time

Compensation: Competitive, based on experience and performance



About the Role



We are looking for a highly detail-oriented Real Estate Research Specialist with strong English skills to support U.S. property research and outbound calling. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys investigative work, accuracy, and structured processes, and is comfortable speaking professionally with U.S. property owners.



This position combines online research with outbound calling and appointment setting.



Responsibilities



Research foreclosure, tax deed, and property records using U.S. county websites



Collect, verify, and organize property and ownership data



Identify qualified leads based on clear criteria



Make outbound calls using provided scripts



Communicate clearly and professionally with property owners



Set appointments and pass qualified leads



Maintain accurate records in spreadsheets or CRM systems



Requirements



Strong to exceptional English (written and spoken)



Ability to work at a college-educated level



Clear speaking voice; neutral accent preferred



Comfortable with outbound calling



Strong reading comprehension and attention to detail



Reliable, organized, and consistent



Stable internet and quiet work environment



Preferred (Not Required)



Experience with U.S. public records or real estate research



Appointment setting or sales experience



Familiarity with Google Sheets, Excel, or CRM tools



Hiring Process



Qualified applicants will complete a short skills and comprehension quiz assessing:



English comprehension



Research ability



Attention to detail



Professional judgment



Only applicants who complete the quiz will be considered.



How to Apply



Please submit:



A short professional introduction



Relevant experience (research and/or calling)



Confirmation of English proficiency



(Optional) Short voice recording
